task: Ralph (2026-05-15, R16): User created a markdown document project in Doable and sees it loaded in the preview iframe, but cannot scroll down within the preview to view the full content. Reproduce on dev via claude-in-chrome, root-cause it (preview iframe scroll surface — likely CSS overflow or iframe sizing issue), fix it, commit, push, and deploy to dev. Never bind 0.0.0.0. Never weaken security. Never touch prod.

stories.title: Reproduce the preview scroll bug in claude-in-chrome
acceptanceCriteria: Located 'Make A Simple Markdown About Spots' (id 67deca59-c8a0-48d9-9e54-bf62457be0ce) in dashboard
Opened in editor — markdown rendered 'Best Spots to Visit in Italy' through 'Rome' but cut off; content continues beyond viewport
Mousewheel scroll on iframe area produced no movement; bodyScrollHeight (2107) >> bodyClientHeight when measured standalone confirmed content overflows
Iframe attrs captured: width 812, height 553, overflow clip, scrolling=null, sandbox=allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-forms allow-popups allow-modals; parent overflow:hidden, parentH=553
Bug reproducible — confirmed by scrolling test in editor (no change between pre/post screenshots)

stories.title: Root-cause the scroll bug (no guessing)
acceptanceCriteria: Owner: mcp-servers/markdown-builder/index.mjs:181 bakes `body { ... overflow: hidden }` into the generated index.html for every markdown project

Cause: standalone the browser window scrollbar handles overflow regardless of body overflow:hidden — bug invisible to direct-page testing. Embedded as iframe, scroll relies on the inner doc's body/html overflow chain, which body{overflow:hidden} explicitly blocks

Two evidence sources: (a) DOM probe via javascript_tool reported bodyOverflow:'hidden', bodyOverflowY:'hidden', htmlScroll:2107 vs htmlClient:617 — overflow ratio confirms scrollable content with hidden body; (b) grep -rn overflow on the deployed project files at /root/doable/services/api/projects/67deca59-.../index.html showed the literal `overflow: hidden` rule in the body declaration
No recent regression — this has been in the markdown-builder template since it was added; the bug surfaced because the user used the markdown tool path which had never been iframe-tested

stories.title: Fix the scroll surface + verify locally / on dev
acceptanceCriteria: Minimal change: removed ` overflow: hidden;` segment from body rule on line 181 of mcp-servers/markdown-builder/index.mjs (1 line, +1 -1). `pre { overflow: auto }` preserved for wide code blocks
TypeScript not applicable — .mjs file, no compile step. Validated by inspecting the diff

Re-tested AFTER deploy in claude-in-chrome: preview iframe now scrolls — confirmed new content ('Venice', 'Piazza San Marco', 'The Amalfi Coast', 'Cinque Terre') visible after 10-tick scroll where previously only 'Best Spots / Rome / Vatican' was reachable
Vite-React preview from R15 fix still works (no regression — Smoke Vite Updated rendered correctly during this session)
No 0.0.0.0 binds introduced (no service binding touched)

stories.title: Commit, push to main, deploy on dev, final smoke
acceptanceCriteria: Atomic commit on fix/r16-markdown-preview-scroll branch — see commit 72951def (squashed into main as c83cc2f5)
PR #46 opened + admin-merged squash to main

dodev.fid.pw: sudo -u doable git pull --ff-only origin main applied c83cc2f5; one-off sed patch on existing project index.html so the user's existing markdown sees the fix without re-running the build tool
Final claude-in-chrome smoke: markdown preview scrolls (Venice section visible post-scroll); Vite-React preview from R15 fix still renders correctly
Before/after screenshots captured during this session (ss_5060h228r before, ss_31329qxz6 after) show different content reachable post-scroll
